name: A. Проектирование production-RAG пайплайна
intended_goal: Understand / design an end-to-end production RAG pipeline over research papers

messages.text: I'm building a production RAG system over a set of research papers. My goal is to understand and design the full pipeline end to end. What chunking strategies should I consider for indexing?
We'll fix fixed-size chunking with overlap as our chunking approach. What is the role of indexing in the pre-retrieval stage?
After retrieval, what re-ranking techniques are used in the post-retrieval stage?
Before we continue — remind me what our goal is and what we've fixed so far.
For the generation part, what is the difference between RAG-Sequence and RAG-Token models?
Given our production goal, which of those two is more flexible about mixing documents?
What are the three RAG paradigms described in the survey?
Which of those paradigms best matches our fixed-chunking plus re-ranking setup?
What are the main challenges and future directions we should plan for?
Good. What constraints and terms have we fixed in this conversation so far?
Now summarize the end-to-end pipeline we've designed, consistent with our goal and constraints, and cite sources.

name: B. Улучшение качества retrieval существующего RAG
intended_goal: Improve the retrieval quality of an existing, noisy RAG system

messages.text: I already have a RAG system but its answers are noisy. My goal is to improve its retrieval quality. What evaluation aspects and quality scores are used to evaluate RAG models?
What re-ranking techniques could I add in the post-retrieval stage to reduce noise?
Let's fix a term: by 're-ranking' we mean post-retrieval reordering of chunks. Also, what is our goal again?
What is the role of indexing in the pre-retrieval stage, and could better indexing help our retrieval quality?
What open-domain question answering datasets were used to evaluate the original RAG model?
Of the datasets you listed, which are the most standard for QA benchmarking?
What are the three RAG paradigms, and which one first introduces re-ranking as we defined it?
Remind me: what is our goal, and which terms have we fixed so far?
What challenges and future directions should we watch for while improving retrieval?
Finally, summarize the concrete steps to improve our retrieval quality, consistent with our goal, and cite sources.
